evals.name: bootstrap_unversioned_bundle
evals.description: Tests bootstrapping an existing unversioned skill bundle. The skill should inventory files, assign versions, create MANIFEST.yaml and CHANGELOG.md, add internal headers where safe, and use manifest-only tracking for strict-format files.
evals.prompt: I have a skill project with these files: SKILL.md, evals.json, generate.js, and output.pdf. None of them currently carry version metadata. This is the third major version of the skill — we've been through two previous iterations but never tracked versions. Please version this bundle as 3.0.0.
expectations: MANIFEST.yaml is created listing all 4 user files plus CHANGELOG.md
MANIFEST.yaml is treated as the control file and is not self-listed in files
CHANGELOG.md is created with a 3.0.0 entry summarizing the bootstrap
SKILL.md version 3 is tracked according to the chosen frontmatter_mode
evals.json is tracked in MANIFEST.yaml with version: 3 and file_role: evals
generate.js is tracked in MANIFEST.yaml with version: 3 and file_role: script
output.pdf is tracked in MANIFEST.yaml with a hash but no internal header
All manifest paths are relative, not absolute
skill_bundle name is consistent across all files

evals.name: open_session_verify_bundle
evals.description: Tests the session-opening protocol. Given a versioned bundle with one missing file and one stale file, the skill should flag both issues.
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new session with this skill bundle. Please verify what I've uploaded and tell me what needs attention.
expectations: Skill reads MANIFEST.yaml first
Reports that generate.js is listed in manifest but missing from uploads
Reports that evals.json version (2) is lower than bundle version (3) and flags it as potentially stale
Does not silently proceed past the missing file
Asks the user how to proceed with the discrepancies
Presents issues clearly without unnecessary alarm

evals.name: save_session_update_bundle
evals.description: Tests the session-closing protocol. After changes to SKILL.md and a new script file, the skill should update all versioning artifacts correctly.
evals.prompt: We've finished our changes. SKILL.md was rewritten to add a new Phase 6 and I created a new file called validate.js. Please update the bundle versioning.
expectations: SKILL.md version is incremented according to the bundle's frontmatter_mode
bundle_version in MANIFEST.yaml is incremented
validate.js is added to MANIFEST.yaml with file_role: script
CHANGELOG.md has a new entry at the top describing the changes
Changelog entry notes that evals.json was NOT updated and may be stale
Hashes are recomputed for changed files
Unchanged files retain their previous version numbers

evals.name: conflict_detection_and_resolution
evals.description: Tests conflict detection when version headers disagree with the manifest. The skill should present the conflict clearly and ask for user resolution.
evals.prompt: I'm loading this bundle into a new session. Something might be off with the versions — can you check?
expectations: Detects that SKILL.md internal header says version 5 but MANIFEST.yaml says version 4
Presents both version claims with their change_summary text
Recommends trusting the more recent version_date as default
Asks the user for explicit confirmation before proceeding
Does NOT silently resolve the conflict

evals.name: cross_surface_chat_handoff
evals.description: Tests generating a complete handoff package for a Chat-to-Chat session transition where no filesystem persists. Handoff output is appropriate here because the target surface is stateless.
evals.prompt: I need to close this session and pick up tomorrow in a new Claude Chat. Package everything I need to bring with me.
expectations: Creates or updates handoff.md with current session summary because the user is crossing a stateless chat boundary
handoff.md includes current bundle version, session accomplishments, stale files, and next steps
If handoff.md is written as a bundle artifact, MANIFEST.yaml is updated to include it
All deliverable files are packaged for download
Explicitly tells the user which files they need to save and re-upload next session
Does not assume filesystem persistence between sessions

evals.name: cross_platform_codex_bootstrap
evals.description: Tests bootstrapping a bundle targeting Codex, which rejects SKILL.md frontmatter beyond name and description. The skill should use frontmatter_mode: minimal and omit the metadata block from SKILL.md.
evals.prompt: I want to version this skill bundle. It needs to work on both Claude and Codex. SKILL.md currently has name and description only in its frontmatter. This is version 1.
expectations: MANIFEST.yaml is created with frontmatter_mode: minimal in the compatibility block
SKILL.md does NOT get a metadata block added to its frontmatter
SKILL.md version is tracked only in MANIFEST.yaml, not in the file itself
evals.json is versioned normally in MANIFEST.yaml without requiring a frontmatter header
compatibility.designed_for uses portability-oriented surfaces or capabilities rather than requiring a single exact model target
CHANGELOG.md notes the cross-platform constraint in the bootstrap entry

evals.name: frontmatter_mode_toggle
evals.description: Tests switching a bundle's frontmatter_mode from metadata (metadata block in SKILL.md) to minimal (no metadata block) when the user decides to target additional platforms.
evals.prompt: This bundle currently targets Claude only and has metadata in SKILL.md frontmatter. I need to make it Codex-compatible too. Please update the bundle to use minimal frontmatter mode.
expectations: Removes the metadata block from SKILL.md frontmatter
Preserves name and description in SKILL.md frontmatter
Updates frontmatter_mode from metadata to minimal in MANIFEST.yaml
SKILL.md version information is preserved in MANIFEST.yaml
CHANGELOG.md entry describes the frontmatter mode change and why
No version information is lost in the transition
bundle_version is incremented

evals.name: compatibility_block_generation
evals.description: Tests that the compatibility block in MANIFEST.yaml is correctly populated when the user specifies testing results across platforms and models.
evals.prompt: I just tested this skill on Sonnet 4.5 in Chat and it mostly works but misses staleness detection. I also tested on Codex and it loads fine but doesn't follow the session protocol. Please update the manifest.
expectations: tested_on array in MANIFEST.yaml gets two new entries
Sonnet 4.5 entry has status: partial with notes about staleness detection
Codex entry has status: partial with notes about session protocol
Each entry includes platform, model (or N/A for Codex), surface, status, date, and notes
CHANGELOG.md entry documents the test results
Existing tested_on entries are preserved, not overwritten

evals.name: gemini_cli_compatibility
evals.description: Tests bootstrapping a bundle targeting Gemini CLI, which requires SKILL.md frontmatter limited to name and description. The skill should use frontmatter_mode: minimal and provide Gemini CLI installation guidance.
evals.prompt: I want to version this skill bundle for use with Gemini CLI. SKILL.md has name and description only. This is the first version — call it 1.0.0. I want to install it to ~/.gemini/skills/ when we're done.
expectations: MANIFEST.yaml is created with frontmatter_mode: minimal in the compatibility block
SKILL.md frontmatter is unchanged — no metadata block added
SKILL.md version is tracked only in MANIFEST.yaml, not in the file itself
compatibility.designed_for can describe CLI-oriented surfaces or capabilities without requiring a vendor-specific single target
Install guidance references ~/.gemini/skills/ directory structure
CHANGELOG.md is created with a 1.0.0 bootstrap entry
Manifest and changelog are generated normally alongside the skill files

evals.name: gemini_gems_prompt_extraction
evals.description: Tests generating a Gem update summary when the user maintains a Gemini Gem alongside the versioned bundle. The skill should identify what needs to be updated in the Gem Manager.
evals.prompt: I maintain a Gemini Gem for this skill. The Gem's system prompt is tracked in GEM_INSTRUCTIONS.md. I've updated SKILL.md and GEM_INSTRUCTIONS.md this session. Package the bundle and tell me what I need to update in the Gem Manager.
expectations: Version tracking updated for SKILL.md and GEM_INSTRUCTIONS.md
MANIFEST.yaml and CHANGELOG.md updated normally
Output includes a Gem update section identifying that GEM_INSTRUCTIONS.md changed
Tells the user to copy the updated instructions into the Gem Manager
Identifies which files need to be re-uploaded to the Gem's knowledge base
Does not attempt to automate Gem Manager interaction

evals.name: git_commit_message_generation
evals.description: Tests that the session close protocol provides a ready-to-use commit message when the user indicates they are committing to a git repo. The message should be derived from the changelog entry, with a file only as an optional convenience.
evals.prompt: We're done with changes. I need to commit this bundle to my git repo. Please update the versioning and give me a commit message I can use.
expectations: All versioning artifacts updated normally (headers, manifest, changelog)
A ready-to-use commit message is returned inline by default
Commit message first line follows the format: bundle-name MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH: summary
Commit message body lists each changed file with what changed
Stale files are noted in the commit message
If a git_commit.txt file is created as a convenience artifact, it is NOT added to MANIFEST.yaml
User is told they can paste the message directly into git commit or use git commit -F git_commit.txt if a file was requested

evals.name: handoff_with_per_file_changes
evals.description: Tests that the handoff note includes per-file change summaries so the next session can verify work without re-reading every file.
evals.prompt: I need to close this session and hand off to a new one. I rewrote the Phase 3 instructions in SKILL.md and added two new expectations to eval 4 in evals.json. Package the handoff.
expectations: handoff.md includes per-file change summaries for SKILL.md and evals.json
SKILL.md summary mentions Phase 3 rewrite specifically
evals.json summary mentions the two new expectations in eval 4
Files not changed this session are not listed in per-file summaries
Handoff still includes standard fields: version, accomplishments, stale files, next steps
Per-file summaries are more granular than the changelog entry

evals.name: local_hash_validation
evals.description: Tests awareness of the validate.sh script for local hash verification. The skill should reference it when appropriate, especially for Chat-to-Code transitions.
evals.prompt: I'm about to upload this bundle to a new Claude Chat session. The files have been sitting in my local directory for a week. How can I verify nothing got corrupted before uploading?
expectations: Recommends running validate.sh to verify hashes locally before uploading
Explains that the script checks file hashes against MANIFEST.yaml
Notes that MANIFEST.yaml itself is the control file and is not self-hashed
Notes that this is more reliable than LLM-computed hashes in Chat
Does not attempt to compute hashes itself when the script is available
Explains the exit codes or output format so the user knows what to expect

evals.name: api_surface_awareness
evals.description: Tests awareness that skills deployed via the API (/v1/skills) use epoch-timestamp versioning and do not sync across surfaces. The skill should explain how manifest-based tracking complements API deployment.
evals.prompt: I've been using skill-provenance locally and now I want to deploy this skill via the Claude API. How does version tracking work across the API and my local development?
expectations: Explains that the API uses epoch-timestamp versioning, not semver
Notes that skills uploaded to the API do not sync to claude.ai or Claude Code
Explains that manifest-based tracking handles the development workflow before and across API deployments
Does not claim that manifest versions replace or override API timestamps
Recommends noting API deployment events in the changelog for traceability

evals.name: dependencies_field_usage
evals.description: Tests that the dependencies field in the manifest is correctly populated when a skill depends on other skills or external resources.
evals.prompt: This skill depends on two other skills: 'data-extraction' and 'format-converter'. Please update the manifest to reflect these dependencies.
expectations: A dependencies field is added to MANIFEST.yaml
Both data-extraction and format-converter are listed
CHANGELOG.md entry documents the dependency additions
Dependencies are listed as skill names, not file paths
If no dependencies exist, the field can be omitted or set to an empty list

evals.name: deployment_metadata_drift
evals.description: Tests optional deployment metadata and drift awareness when a local bundle has changed since the last API deployment.
evals.prompt: MANIFEST.yaml says this bundle is at 4.6.0 and deployments.api.version records a Claude API upload from last week. I changed SKILL.md locally but haven't redeployed yet. What is stale and what should I update?
expectations: Explains that the local bundle may now be ahead of the deployed API copy
Treats bundle_version and deployments.api.version as different version systems with different purposes
Does not overwrite or reinterpret the API timestamp as semver
Recommends updating deployment metadata only after an actual redeploy or explicit verification
Recommends noting deployment or non-deployment status in the changelog for traceability

evals.name: multi_surface_install_targets
evals.description: Tests guidance for the same canonical bundle being installed in multiple local skill directories or upload surfaces.
evals.prompt: I keep the same skill in ~/.codex/skills/, .agents/skills/, and a Claude settings ZIP. How should the manifest track that without turning them into separate skills?
expectations: Treats the bundle as one canonical skill with multiple install or deployment targets
Uses optional deployment metadata rather than inventing separate bundle names
Notes that .agents/skills can act as a neutral cross-client install location
Explains that each installed copy can drift independently from the canonical bundle
Preserves bundle_version as the author-side source of truth

evals.name: trust_audit_untrusted_bundle
evals.description: Tests trust and audit guidance for a downloaded bundle before installation or reuse.
evals.prompt: I downloaded a skill bundle from someone else's repo and want to install it across my tools. Before I trust it, what should I verify?
expectations: Recommends reading MANIFEST.yaml to confirm expected files and roles
Recommends verifying hashes locally with validate.sh when available
Recommends reviewing CHANGELOG.md to understand recent changes and stale files
Explains that deployment metadata or install-target notes can reveal which copies may be outdated
Does not treat the bundle as trustworthy solely because it has a manifest

evals.name: deployment_metadata_redeploy_event
evals.description: Tests updating deployment metadata after a real redeploy so author-side semver and platform-native deployment versions remain distinct but traceable.
evals.prompt: I updated this bundle locally, then redeployed it through the Claude API just now. Please update the bundle records so the deployment history is accurate.
expectations: Updates deployments.api metadata only because an actual redeploy happened
Keeps bundle_version as semver and does not replace it with the API timestamp
Treats the API version as platform-native deployment metadata, not the bundle's canonical version
Recommends or records a changelog note describing the redeploy event
Does not imply that redeploying automatically changes unchanged file versions

evals.name: deployment_metadata_conflict
evals.description: Tests conflict handling when deployment metadata disagrees with the user's report of what is currently installed or deployed.
evals.prompt: MANIFEST.yaml says deployments.api.version is 1759178010641129, but I think I manually redeployed a newer copy yesterday from another machine. Please fix the manifest.
expectations: Treats the deployment discrepancy as a conflict rather than silently overwriting metadata
Explains which value comes from the manifest and which comes from the user's report
Asks for confirmation or supporting details before changing deployment metadata
Does not reinterpret platform-native deployment versions as semver
Recommends recording the resolution in the changelog once confirmed

evals.name: hash_mismatch_untrusted_bundle
evals.description: Tests that a hash mismatch in a downloaded bundle is treated as a verification failure, not merely ordinary staleness.
evals.prompt: I downloaded this skill bundle from another repo. MANIFEST.yaml and CHANGELOG.md are present, but one file fails hash verification. What should I do before installing it?
expectations: Treats the hash mismatch as a verification problem or trust issue, not just a stale-file warning
Recommends verifying the bundle locally with validate.sh when available
Explains that the manifest helps detect tampering or accidental drift but does not guarantee trust by itself
Avoids presenting the mismatched bundle as safely installable without clarification
Recommends checking changelog intent and source provenance before proceeding

evals.name: packaged_subset_vs_canonical_bundle
evals.description: Tests awareness that a .skill or .zip installable package may contain only the packaged subset while the canonical source bundle tracks additional files.
evals.prompt: I opened a .skill ZIP that has SKILL.md, README.md, and references, but MANIFEST.yaml says the canonical bundle also includes evals and scripts that are not in this package. Is something wrong?
expectations: Explains the difference between an installable packaged subset and the canonical source bundle
Does not silently treat the package as complete if the manifest expects additional tracked files
Flags the missing tracked files clearly and asks how the user wants to proceed
Explains that some files may travel separately even when the installed skill loads correctly
Uses the manifest as the authoritative inventory rather than the archive contents alone

evals.name: settings_ui_skill_zip_round_trip
evals.description: Tests that a .skill ZIP exported from Claude Settings can be re-imported without losing version identity or integrity. Verifies the round-trip preserves manifest, changelog, and hash-verifiable state.
evals.prompt: I exported this skill as a .skill ZIP from Claude Settings, then re-imported it into a different account. The ZIP contains SKILL.md, README.md, MANIFEST.yaml, CHANGELOG.md, and references/. Please verify the imported bundle is intact and matches the manifest.
expectations: Reads MANIFEST.yaml and checks all listed files against the imported contents
Verifies hashes for files present in the import
Notes any files listed in the manifest that are absent from the .skill ZIP (e.g., evals, scripts) and explains they may travel separately from the installable package
Does not treat a packaged subset as a corruption or integrity failure
Confirms the bundle version and changelog are intact
Explains that .skill is a standard ZIP and can be renamed to .zip for platforms that require it

evals.name: copilot_vscode_skill_bootstrap
evals.description: Tests bootstrapping a versioned bundle for use with GitHub Copilot in VS Code, which discovers skills in .github/skills/ or ~/.agents/skills/.
evals.prompt: I want to version this skill for GitHub Copilot in VS Code. The skill lives in .github/skills/my-skill/ with SKILL.md and a references/ folder. This is version 1.0.0.
expectations: MANIFEST.yaml is created following the standard bootstrap protocol
Uses frontmatter_mode: minimal or follows the agentskills.io spec for Copilot compatibility
Does not add platform-specific frontmatter fields that Copilot would reject
CHANGELOG.md is created with a 1.0.0 bootstrap entry
Manifest paths are relative to the skill directory, not the repo root
Notes that the same bundle can be installed to ~/.agents/skills/ for personal scope

evals.name: cursor_skill_bootstrap
evals.description: Tests bootstrapping a versioned bundle for use with Cursor, which discovers skills in .cursor/skills/ and follows the agentskills.io spec.
evals.prompt: I'm building a skill for Cursor. It lives in .cursor/skills/my-skill/ with SKILL.md and a scripts/ folder. Please bootstrap versioning at 1.0.0.
expectations: MANIFEST.yaml is created following the standard bootstrap protocol
Uses frontmatter_mode compatible with Cursor (follows agentskills.io spec)
scripts/setup.sh is tracked in the manifest with role: script
CHANGELOG.md is created with a 1.0.0 bootstrap entry
Does not assume Claude-specific features or frontmatter extensions
Manifest and changelog live alongside SKILL.md in the skill directory

evals.name: cowork_filesystem_persistence
evals.description: Tests that the session protocol works correctly in a Cowork environment where the filesystem persists between sessions, making handoff notes unnecessary.
evals.prompt: I'm using this skill in Claude Cowork. The bundle is in my project directory and persists between sessions. I just finished updating SKILL.md. Please close the session and update versioning.
expectations: Updates version headers, manifest, and changelog normally
Does NOT create a handoff note because the filesystem persists
Does not ask the user to save or re-upload files
Treats the session close as a local file update, not a stateless handoff
Notes any stale files (e.g., evals.json if not updated) in the changelog
Recognizes that the manifest and changelog in the directory are sufficient context for the next session

evals.name: validate_command_only
evals.description: Tests the dedicated validate workflow. The skill should run or instruct validate.sh and avoid the broader open or close protocols.
evals.prompt: Please only validate this skill bundle. I do not want a session-opening review or any version updates.
expectations: Uses validate.sh when available or computes hash comparisons directly
Reports file count, missing files, hash mismatches, skipped files, and pass/fail status
Does not summarize recent changelog entries as an open-session review
Does not update hashes, bump versions, edit CHANGELOG.md, or create a handoff note
Explains that validation is an integrity check, not a trust anchor

evals.name: complementary_tool_positioning
evals.description: Tests explaining how Skill Provenance complements GitHub gh skill, ClawHub, Skillman, and platform APIs instead of replacing them.
evals.prompt: If GitHub gh skill, ClawHub, Skillman, and the Claude Skills API already track versions, why do I need Skill Provenance?
expectations: Explains that GitHub gh skill tracks source refs, tree SHAs, pinning, and upstream changes
Explains that ClawHub and registries track discovery, publishing, install trust, and registry versions
Explains that Skillman and package managers track consumer-side installs and locks
Explains that platform APIs track deployed surface versions
Positions Skill Provenance as the author-side bundle-local layer for integrity, per-file staleness, changelog, hashes, and cross-surface drift
Does not claim competing tools are useless or obsolete

evals.name: integrity_not_trust_anchor
evals.description: Tests the trust model when a user asks whether matching hashes prove a bundle is safe.
evals.prompt: validate.sh passes on this downloaded skill. Does that mean it is safe to install?
expectations: States that matching hashes prove the files match the recorded manifest state
States that this is an integrity check, not a trust anchor
Explains that the manifest does not prove author identity, cryptographically sign the bundle, scan for malware, or sandbox runtime behavior
Recommends checking source, publisher, git history, registry page, and changelog before installing
Avoids presenting hash verification as a complete security review

evals.name: validate_derived_copy_without_false_corruption
evals.description: Tests validating a strict-platform or registry copy as its own package rather than treating missing canonical development files as corruption.
evals.prompt: This strict-platform copy only contains SKILL.md, README.md, MANIFEST.yaml, and evals. Please validate it and tell me if anything is wrong.
expectations: Uses the copy's own MANIFEST.yaml as the authoritative inventory
Identifies the copy as a derived strict-platform install copy or registry package when frontmatter_mode or contents indicate that
Does not treat missing development-only files as corruption unless this manifest lists them
Reports hash and missing-file status for the files listed in this manifest
Recommends returning to the canonical source bundle before editing, repackaging, or republishing

evals.name: missing_or_malformed_manifest_hash_fails_closed
evals.description: Tests that verify mode rejects implicit hash omissions and malformed SHA-256 values instead of silently skipping tracked entries.
evals.prompt: In a disposable copy, remove one tracked file's hash field and replace another with sha256:not-a-hash. Run verify-only validation and report whether the bundle passes.
expectations: Runs the negative test in a disposable copy and leaves the canonical bundle unchanged
Reports both the missing hash and malformed hash as manifest errors tied to their paths
Exits nonzero even when every present file would otherwise match
Does not count either invalid entry as an intentional skip
Does not print All hashes verified or otherwise report success
Accepts a canonical hash only when it is sha256 followed by exactly 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters

evals.name: explicit_null_hash_opt_out
evals.description: Tests that hash verification can be intentionally disabled only with an explicit hash: null marker and that the opt-out remains visible in validation results.
evals.prompt: A tracked source file is intentionally not hash-pinned and its manifest entry says hash: null. Validate the bundle and explain the result.
expectations: Recognizes hash: null as an explicit opt-out rather than a malformed or missing field
Reports the path as skipped with an explicit no-hash or opt-out reason
Does not treat version: null by itself as permission to omit the hash field
Continues validating every other manifest-listed file
Reports checked, skipped, and error counts accurately
Does not claim every file was hash-verified when one entry explicitly opted out

evals.name: update_mode_repairs_missing_or_malformed_hashes
evals.description: Tests that update mode recovers missing and malformed hash entries when the corresponding files are available.
evals.prompt: In a disposable bundle, remove one tracked hash field and shorten another SHA-256 value, then run validate.sh --update followed by verify-only validation.
expectations: Runs against a disposable copy and leaves the canonical bundle unchanged
Recomputes and inserts a complete hash for each available file whose hash is missing or malformed
Writes sha256 followed by exactly 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters
Preserves explicit hash: null opt-outs rather than repairing them
A subsequent verify-only run checks all non-opted-out entries and exits successfully
Preserves unrelated manifest content and file ordering
Does not use update mode to conceal missing files

evals.name: validated_against_attestation_reporting
evals.description: Tests that an optional validated_against block is parsed and reported as attestation bound to the exact bundle_version, distinct from compatibility.tested_on.
evals.prompt: This bundle's MANIFEST.yaml has a validated_against entry whose bundle_version matches the current bundle_version. Run validate.sh and explain what the ATTEST output means, including how it differs from the tested_on compatibility list.
expectations: Runs validate.sh and reads the ATTEST line after the hash results
Explains that the entry attests the exact current bundle_version on a named harness and model
Distinguishes validated_against (release-bound attestation) from compatibility.tested_on (unbound design-time compatibility claims)
Distinguishes attestation from integrity: hashes answer whether the bytes match, attestation answers whether the bytes were validated on a given environment
Does not claim that a pass attestation guarantees behavior on other harnesses or models

evals.name: stale_attestation_is_informational_not_gating
evals.description: Tests that a stale or absent validated_against record is reported without changing validate.sh exit semantics, and that the agent recommends re-validation rather than rejection.
evals.prompt: In a disposable bundle copy, bump bundle_version in MANIFEST.yaml so no validated_against entry matches it, keep all file hashes correct, then run validate.sh and interpret the result.
expectations: Runs against a disposable copy and leaves the canonical bundle unchanged
Observes the ATTEST stale line naming the current bundle_version and the latest recorded attestation
Confirms exit code 0 because all hashes verify: attestation never gates integrity
Recommends re-validating on a current harness/model pair and recording a new validated_against entry, not rejecting the bundle
Does not move environment or attestation claims into hash fields or invent a failing exit code for staleness
